title: Model Version Tracking and Immutable Version IDs (Art. 13)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Art.13.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that EU AI Act Art.13 (Transparency and information) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.21.7: Model Version Tracking and Immutable Version IDs (Art. 13).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.21.7' running on index=ai_serving — model_version, deployment, traffic_pct, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=ai_serving sourcetype="ai:gateway:access" earliest=-4h
| eval semver_ok=if(match(model_version,"^v?\d+\.\d+\.\d+"),1,0)
| stats count as reqs, sum(semver_ok) as semver_hits by deployment, model_version
| eval semver_pct=round(100*semver_hits/reqs,2)
| where isnull(model_version) OR model_version="" OR semver_ok=0
| sort - reqs
description: Detects production traffic served from non-semver or missing model_version to support traceability and change control evidence.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ai_serving and sourcetypes ai:gateway:access. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Enforce semver in model registry webhook; (2) block deploy if version missing; (3) alert on null model_version > 10/min; (4) map deployment to ITSI entity. Indexes required: `index=ai_serving`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ai:gateway:access`.
visualization: Pie chart (semver_ok), Table (bad versions), Column chart (reqs by deployment)
knownFalsePositives: High-risk system events during model retraining, scheduled validation runs, or feature rollouts that are pre-approved in the model risk register.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ai_serving` — model_version, deployment, traffic_pct.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enforce semver in model registry webhook; (2) block deploy if version missing; (3) alert on null model_version > 10/min; (4) map deployment to ITSI entity.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ai_serving sourcetype="ai:gateway:access" earliest=-4h
| eval semver_ok=if(match(model_version,"^v?\d+\.\d+\.\d+"),1,0)
| stats count as reqs, sum(semver_ok) as semver_hits by deployment, model_version
| eval semver_pct=round(100*semver_hits/reqs,2)
| where isnull(model_version) OR model_version="" OR semver_ok=0
| sort - reqs
```

Understanding this SPL

**Model Version Tracking and Immutable Version IDs (Art. 13) (EU AI Act)** — Detects production traffic served from non-semver or missing model_version to support traceability and change control evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ai_serving` — model_version, deployment, traffic_pct. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ai_serving; **sourcetype**: ai:gateway:access. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ai_serving, sourcetype="ai:gateway:access", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **semver_ok** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by deployment, model_version** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **semver_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(model_version) OR model_version="" OR semver_ok=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the AI model registry, MLOps pipeline, and the ServiceNow GRC console for the same validation window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Pie chart (semver_ok), Table (bad versions), Column chart (reqs by deployment)
grandmaExplanation: We look for production traffic served from non-semver or missing model_version to support traceability and change control evidence.
